events.text: Protecting repositories requires an external storage destination for incremental backup transfers. We start by checking which storage providers -- S3, B2, or remote machine -- are configured.
Storage imported. We list all configured backends to confirm the S3 destination is registered and ready for backup transfers.
Next we create a backup strategy -- setting the destination to our S3 backend, scheduling daily runs at two AM, and enabling it immediately.
Strategy saved. We inspect the full configuration to verify the schedule, destination, and enabled status before moving on.
With backups configured, we set up the machine's infrastructure -- public IP, base domain, and certificate email for automatic TLS provisioning.
Now we open the network ports the machine needs -- TCP ports for mail protocols and a UDP port for DNS resolution.
We review the full infrastructure configuration for the machine -- IP address, domain, certificate email, and all port mappings in one view.
Finally, we disable the backup strategy without removing it. The configuration stays saved so it can be re-enabled at any time.
Confirmed -- the strategy is disabled while the destination and schedule remain intact. Re-enable with a single flag when you are ready.
